content.full_text:
Fern PoLrrrcAL PnecrrcEs CovrMrs s roN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
May 15,2003
Stephen Ross
City of San Diego
1200 Third Avenue, Suite 1100
San Diego, CA92l0l
Re: Your Request for'Wriffen Advice
Our File No. .4.-03-040
Dear Mr. Ross:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the City of San
Diego regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTION
Would the City of San Diego's adoption of an ordinance allowing elected city
officials and candidates for elective city office to establish a separate bank account for
contributions to a legal defense fund impermissibly conflict with Govemment Code
section 85201?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Such an ordinance would conflict with the state law requirementthat a
candidate may only set up one bank account and one committee per election.
FACTS
The City of San Diego Ethics Commission has been tasked with reviewing the
city's election campaign control ordinance and is presently in the process of drafting
amendments that will be proposed to the city council for consideration. One such
amendment would provide that elected city officers and candidates for elective city ofEce
would be allowed to open a bank account, separate and distinct from their campaign bank
account, for the pu{pose of accepting funds to pay for a legal defense. These funds would
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
!:E


File No. A-03-040
Page No. 2
be solely to defray attomey's fees and other legal costs incurred in the officer,s or
cardidate's legal defense to a civil, criminal or administrative proceeding arising directly
out of the conduct of an election campaign, the electoral process, or the performance of
an official's govemmental activities and duties. For reasons relating to bookkeeping
efficiency, auditing concerns, the existence of the city's stringent co-ntribution limiti *d
a municipal interest in maintaining transparency in the campaign fundraising process, it is
not desirable for city officers and candidates to have common iampaign confibutions co-
mingled with contributions made specifically for a legal defense fund.
The San Diego Ethics Commission wishes to establish a procedure for allowing
locally elected officers and candidates access to the same legal defense options that state
officers and candidates enjoy under $ 85304. You are concimed, however, that the "one
bank account" restriction of Government Code section 85201appears to conflict with the
commission's desire to provide a mechanism for legal defense f,5ras that is compatible
with the city's interest in conducting its municipal affairs in a manner that ernbodies clear
and unequivocal standards of disclosure and transparency in government. Thus, you state
that the essence of the issue is whether the City of San Diego, as a charter city, may
permit the collection of contributions in a bank account outside the scope of section
8s201.
ANALYSIS
Section 85201provides that all contributions or loans made to a candidate, or the
candidate's controlled committee, shall be deposited in a single campaign bank account.
This section is known as the "one-bank-account" rule. Statelaw allows for candidates
for elective state offrce or an elected state officer to have officeholder accounts or legal
defense funds. ($ S530a; Reg. 18530.4.) Thelegal defense fund provision of section
85304, however, does not apply to local candidates. ($ 95304, Reg. 1g530.4; In re
Pelham Q00l) 15 FPPC ops. 1; Kawagoe Advice Ltr. No. l,-,oz-log.) As you describe
the city's proposal, such a system of separate bank accounts for legal defense funds in
addition to the candidate's campaign bank account would conflict with section g5201.


File No. 4-03-040
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
By: C.
Counsel, Legal Division
CSTjg

L./

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would the City of San Diego's adoption of an ordinance allowing elected city
officials and candidates for elective city office to establish a separate bank account for
contributions to a legal defense fund impermissibly conflict with Govemment Code
section 85201?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Such an ordinance would conflict with the state law requirementthat a
candidate may only set up one bank account and one committee per election.
